src/fetch/Fetch.ts.summary: Core async fetch engine: prepares request, applies interceptor/cache/preload/virtual-server/CORS-preflight checks, performs Node http/https request, decodes gzip/deflate/br body streams, follows redirects, and resolves a Response. Registers/ends an AsyncTaskManager task per request and wires signal-abort and socket-close handling.
src/fetch/Fetch.ts.inputs: Options { browserFrame, window, url|Request, init, redirectCount, contentType, disableCache/SameOriginPolicy/Preload, unfilteredHeaders }.
src/fetch/Fetch.ts.outputs: Promise<Response>; throws DOMException on network/security/abort/redirect errors.
src/fetch/Fetch.ts.should: Drive one async HTTP request lifecycle end to end; track it in asyncTaskManager; abort/destroy node request+response and cancel body on signal/manager abort; recurse for redirects and cache revalidation.
src/fetch/Fetch.ts.shouldNot: Do synchronous I/O; be reused for more than one send(); leak the abort listener or a task on any exit path.

src/fetch/SyncFetch.ts.summary: Synchronous fetch engine mirroring Fetch: runs the HTTP request in a spawned child Node process via SyncFetchScriptBuilder + execFileSync, decodes the body synchronously, handles cache revalidation (sync + async background), virtual server, CORS preflight, and redirects. Rejects stream request bodies.
src/fetch/SyncFetch.ts.inputs: Options { browserFrame, window, url|Request, init, redirectCount, contentType, disableCache/SameOriginPolicy, unfilteredHeaders }.
src/fetch/SyncFetch.ts.should: Block the calling thread to produce a response; reuse cache/preflight/interceptor logic in a sync shape; store results in the response cache.
src/fetch/SyncFetch.ts.shouldNot: Accept a ReadableStream request body; register async task-manager tasks for its own blocking request.

src/fetch/Request.ts.summary: WHATWG Request implementation. Normalizes method/mode/credentials/redirect/referrer/signal/URL from input+init, builds the body stream/buffer via FetchBodyUtility, strips forbidden headers, and exposes body-reading methods (arrayBuffer/blob/buffer/text/json/formData) that consume the stream under an AsyncTaskManager task.
src/fetch/Request.ts.inputs: input: string|URL|Request, init?: IRequestInit; window injected by class extender.
src/fetch/Request.ts.outputs: Request instance; body accessors return Promises; clone() returns a copy.
src/fetch/Request.ts.should: Validate method/body/URL/referrerPolicy/redirect; mark bodyUsed once consumed; abort signal + set aborted on task abort.
src/fetch/Request.ts.shouldNot: Be constructed outside a Window context; allow a body to be read twice; perform network I/O.

src/fetch/Response.ts.summary: WHATWG Response implementation. Holds status/headers/body stream, exposes body-reading methods (arrayBuffer/blob/buffer/text/json/formData) that consume the stream under an AsyncTaskManager task and backfill the response cache. Provides static redirect/error/json constructors and clone via stream tee.
src/fetch/Response.ts.inputs: body?: TResponseBody, init?: IResponseInit; window injected by class extender.
src/fetch/Response.ts.outputs: Response instance; body accessors return Promises.
src/fetch/Response.ts.should: Delete Set-Cookie headers; mark bodyUsed once; return empty result when browser frame is torn down; store consumed buffer into cachedResponse.
src/fetch/Response.ts.shouldNot: Be constructed outside a Window context; allow body reuse; perform network I/O.

src/fetch/Headers.ts.summary: WHATWG Headers map. Case-insensitive storage keyed by lowercased name, with append/delete/get/set/has, getSetCookie, and iterators (keys/values/entries/forEach). Comma-joins multi-value headers on read.
src/fetch/Headers.ts.inputs: init?: THeadersInit (Headers | array of pairs | record).
src/fetch/Headers.ts.outputs: Header string values, iterators, boolean has.
src/fetch/Headers.ts.should: Preserve original-cased name while matching case-insensitively; keep multiple appended values; validate array entry shape.
src/fetch/Headers.ts.shouldNot: Perform network I/O or enforce forbidden-header policy (that lives in the request/response utilities).

src/fetch/AbortController.ts.summary: WHATWG AbortController. Owns an AbortSignal and forwards abort(reason) to the signal's internal abort. Window injected by class extender.
src/fetch/AbortController.ts.shouldNot: Hold request state or perform I/O.

src/fetch/AbortSignal.ts.summary: WHATWG AbortSignal EventTarget. Tracks aborted/reason, dispatches an 'abort' event on internal abort, and provides throwIfAborted plus static abort/timeout/any combinators. Read-only aborted/reason getters.
src/fetch/AbortSignal.ts.should: Fire abort only once; set a default DOMException reason; auto-abort on timeout; propagate from any() source signals and stop listening after first.
src/fetch/AbortSignal.ts.shouldNot: Allow public writes to aborted/reason; abort twice.

src/fetch/ResourceFetch.ts.summary: Helper for fetching page sub-resources (script/style/module) both async (Fetch) and sync (SyncFetch). Checks the document preload cache first, disables same-origin policy for scripts/styles, and returns resource content + any virtual-server file path.
src/fetch/ResourceFetch.ts.inputs: url, destination ('script'|'style'|'module'), options { credentials, referrerPolicy }; window.
src/fetch/ResourceFetch.ts.outputs: IResourceFetchResponse { content, virtualServerFile }; throws DOMException on non-ok.
src/fetch/ResourceFetch.ts.should: Consume matching preload entries; return empty result if browser frame is gone.
src/fetch/ResourceFetch.ts.shouldNot: Follow the preload path for modules; leave preload entries in the map after use.

src/fetch/utilities/FetchBodyUtility.ts.summary: Body stream helpers. getBodyStream normalizes any TRequestBody/TResponseBody into { stream, buffer, contentType, contentLength }; cloneBodyStream tees/pipes a body; consumeBodyStream reads a ReadableStream to a Buffer honoring aborted/error flags; toReadableStream/nodeToWebStream wrap values and Node streams.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchBodyUtility.ts.inputs: Body values, window, request/response-like objects with body/aborted/error.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchBodyUtility.ts.should: Throw on cloning an already-used body; surface aborted/error during consumption as DOMException; attach nodeStream symbol for later cloning.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchBodyUtility.ts.shouldNot: Perform network requests or mutate headers.

src/fetch/utilities/FetchCORSUtility.ts.summary: Determines whether a request is cross-origin by comparing hostname (with suffix allowance) and protocol of origin vs target URL; treats about:/javascript: targets as non-CORS.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchCORSUtility.ts.should: Return false for about:/javascript: schemes; compare protocol and hostname.

src/fetch/utilities/FetchRequestHeaderUt.summary: Request header policy. removeForbiddenHeaders strips forbidden/proxy-/sec- headers; isHeaderForbidden checks the list; getRequestHeaders builds the final Node header map, adding Accept-Encoding/Connection/User-Agent/Referer/Accept/Content-Length/Content-Type, attaching cookies per credentials mode, and Origin for CORS.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchRequestHeaderUt.inputs: { browserFrame, window, request, baseHeaders }; Headers to sanitize.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchRequestHeaderUt.should: Respect credentials mode for cookie inclusion; add Origin only when CORS.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchRequestHeaderUt.shouldNot: Send network requests; retain forbidden headers.

src/fetch/utilities/FetchResponseHeaderU.summary: Parses raw Node response header pairs into a Headers object, diverting Set-Cookie/Set-Cookie2 into the browser context's cookie container instead of exposing them on the response.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchResponseHeaderU.should: Add parsed cookies to the cookieContainer; append all other headers.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchResponseHeaderU.shouldNot: Expose Set-Cookie headers on the Headers object.

src/fetch/utilities/FetchRequestReferrer.summary: Referrer-policy engine. prepareRequest defaults the policy and computes the sent referrer; getInitialReferrer normalizes init referrer; getReferrerPolicyFromHeader parses Referrer-Policy; getSentReferrer applies the policy (origin/strict/same-origin/etc) and trustworthiness checks to produce the Referer value.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchRequestReferrer.should: Implement the referrer-policy spec including trustworthy-origin downgrade rules.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchRequestReferrer.shouldNot: Perform network I/O; leak credentials/hash in referrer.

src/fetch/utilities/FetchRequestValidati.summary: Static validators for request method (rejects TRACE/TRACK/CONNECT and non-uppercase), body (no body on GET/HEAD), URL (no embedded credentials), referrer policy, redirect value, and supported URL schema (data/http/https). Throws DOMException on failure.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchRequestValidati.should: Enumerate and reject each invalid case explicitly.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchRequestValidati.shouldNot: Mutate the request or perform I/O.

src/fetch/utilities/FetchResponseRedirec.summary: Redirect helpers: isRedirect checks 301/302/303/307/308; isMaxRedirectsReached compares count against a max of 20.
src/fetch/utilities/FetchResponseRedirec.should: Encapsulate redirect status codes and the max-redirect limit.

src/fetch/utilities/VirtualServerUtility.summary: Maps request URLs to filesystem paths for configured virtual servers and node-module resolution, and builds 404 (async Response / sync ISyncResponse) not-found results. Reads browser settings via WindowBrowserContext.
src/fetch/utilities/VirtualServerUtility.should: Match string-prefix and RegExp virtual server URLs and translate to on-disk paths.
src/fetch/utilities/VirtualServerUtility.shouldNot: Read files itself (the fetch classes do the FS read).

src/fetch/utilities/SyncFetchScriptBuild.summary: Builds the Node script string executed in a child process for synchronous fetch: constructs http/https options (headers, cert/key, rejectUnauthorized), sends the request, and prints a JSON blob with statusCode/message/rawHeaders/base64 body or an error.
src/fetch/utilities/SyncFetchScriptBuild.should: Serialize request faithfully and base64-encode body/response for transport.
src/fetch/utilities/SyncFetchScriptBuild.shouldNot: Execute the request itself (SyncFetch runs it).

src/fetch/multipart/MultipartFormDataPar.summary: Multipart form-data bridge. streamToFormData reads a body ReadableStream through MultipartReader honoring aborted/error to yield FormData+buffer; formDataToStream serializes a FormData into a multipart body Buffer/stream with a generated boundary and content headers.
src/fetch/multipart/MultipartFormDataPar.outputs: { formData, buffer } / { contentType, contentLength, buffer, stream }.
src/fetch/multipart/MultipartFormDataPar.should: Validate multipart content-type and boundary; surface abort/error during read.

src/fetch/multipart/MultipartReader.ts.summary: Incremental byte-level multipart parser. write() feeds chunks through a boundary/header/data state machine accumulating parts; end() flushes trailing data; appends string values or File objects to a FormData.
src/fetch/multipart/MultipartReader.ts.should: Parse content-disposition/content-type per part and build File for filename parts.
src/fetch/multipart/MultipartReader.ts.shouldNot: Read from a stream directly (parser owns bytes only, not I/O).

src/fetch/data-uri/DataURIParser.ts.summary: Parses a data: URI into { type, charset, buffer }, handling base64 vs ascii encoding, charset metadata, and US-ASCII defaults.
src/fetch/data-uri/DataURIParser.ts.outputs: { type, charset, buffer }; throws TypeError on malformed input.
src/fetch/data-uri/DataURIParser.ts.shouldNot: Handle non-data URIs or perform I/O.

src/fetch/certificate/FetchHTTPSCertific.summary: Static bundled self-signed HTTPS certificate and private key used by Fetch/SyncFetch for TLS on outgoing https requests.
src/fetch/certificate/FetchHTTPSCertific.shouldNot: Be used for real production trust.

src/fetch/preload/PreloadEntry.ts.summary: Represents an in-flight or completed preload (<link rel=preload>) resource. Holds response/error/integrity and exposes onResponseAvailable() (a Promise resolved by responseAvailable() when the fetch finishes).
src/fetch/preload/PreloadEntry.ts.should: Resolve or reject the pending callback exactly once when the response arrives.

src/fetch/preload/PreloadUtility.ts.summary: Computes the string key (JSON of url/destination/mode/credentialsMode) used to index the document's preload map.
src/fetch/preload/PreloadUtility.ts.should: Produce a stable key matching how fetch/resource lookups build it.

src/fetch/cache/response/ResponseCache.t.summary: In-memory HTTP response cache keyed by URL with Vary/method matching. Implements Cache-Control (max-age/no-cache/no-store/must-revalidate/stale-while-revalidate), Expires, Last-Modified, ETag, and 304 revalidation updates; marks entries fresh/stale; get/add/clear.
src/fetch/cache/response/ResponseCache.t.should: Only cache GET/HEAD; honor no-cache/no-store; expire and revalidate correctly; own a ResponseCacheFileSystem.
src/fetch/cache/response/ResponseCache.t.shouldNot: Perform network I/O; cache non-cacheable methods.

src/fetch/cache/response/ResponseCacheFi.summary: Persists/loads the response cache to/from disk. load reads .json metadata + .data body files into the entries map; save writes non-virtual entries as md5-hashed json+data files, deduplicating by hash.
src/fetch/cache/response/ResponseCacheFi.should: Skip virtual entries; tolerate missing directories/files; rebuild Headers on load.
src/fetch/cache/response/ResponseCacheFi.shouldNot: Persist virtual-server responses or response bodies inline in json.

src/fetch/cache/preflight/PreflightRespo.summary: In-memory CORS preflight cache keyed by URL. Stores allowOrigin/allowMethods with an expiry from Access-Control-Max-Age; get evicts expired entries; add validates status/headers/no-cache; clear empties it.
src/fetch/cache/preflight/PreflightRespo.should: Only cache successful preflights with max-age and allow-origin; expire correctly.

src/fetch/cache/response/CachedResponseS.summary: Enum of cached-response freshness states: 'fresh' and 'stale'.

src/fetch/types/IFetchInterceptor.ts.summary: Interface for a fetch interceptor with optional beforeAsyncRequest/beforeSyncRequest/afterAsyncResponse/afterSyncResponse hooks used by Fetch/SyncFetch to modify or short-circuit requests and responses.

src/fetch/types/IRequestInit.ts.summary: Type for Request/fetch init options: body, headers, method, mode, redirect, signal, referrer, credentials, referrerPolicy.

src/fetch/types/IResponseInit.ts.summary: Type for Response init options: headers, status, statusText.

src/fetch/types/ISyncResponse.ts.summary: Type for the plain-object synchronous response returned by SyncFetch: status, statusText, ok, url, redirected, headers, body Buffer, and optional virtualServerFile symbol.

src/fetch/types/IResourceFetchResponse.t.summary: Type returned by ResourceFetch: { content, virtualServerFile }.
src/fetch/types/IResourceFetchResponse.t.should: Describe the resource fetch result shape.

src/fetch/types/TRequestBody.ts.summary: Union type of accepted request bodies: ArrayBuffer, ArrayBufferView, ReadableStream, string, URLSearchParams, Blob, FormData, or null.

src/fetch/types/TResponseBody.ts.summary: Union type of accepted response bodies passed to the Response constructor.

src/fetch/types/IVirtualServer.ts.summary: Type describing a configured virtual server: { url: string|RegExp, directory } mapping requests to a filesystem directory.

src/async-task-manager/AsyncTaskManager..summary: Per-BrowserFrame async lifecycle tracker. Registers/ends tasks, timers, and immediates; waitUntilComplete resolves once all are done (using a low-priority setTimeout to flush microtasks); abort/destroy clear timers/immediates and invoke task abort handlers; supports debug tracing of stuck tasks.
src/async-task-manager/AsyncTaskManager..inputs: browserFrame; startTask/endTask, startTimer/endTimer, startImmediate/endImmediate, waitUntilComplete, abort, destroy.
src/async-task-manager/AsyncTaskManager..outputs: task IDs; Promises resolved on completion.
src/async-task-manager/AsyncTaskManager..should: Invoke abort handlers on abort/destroy; refuse new tasks once destroyed; reject waiters on debug timeout.
src/async-task-manager/AsyncTaskManager..shouldNot: Track work after destroy; resolve waiters while tasks/timers remain.

src/async-task-manager/AsyncTaskManagerD.summary: Named Error subclass used solely to capture a stack trace for debug tracing of unfinished async tasks in AsyncTaskManager.
src/async-task-manager/AsyncTaskManagerD.should: Provide a labeled stack for trace output.
src/async-task-manager/AsyncTaskManagerD.shouldNot: Be thrown as a real runtime failure.

src/browser/Browser.ts.summary: Top-level headless Browser. Creates settings and a default BrowserContext, optionally a process-level exception observer; exposes newPage/newIncognitoContext, waitUntilComplete, abort, and close (which aborts and destroys all contexts).
src/browser/Browser.ts.should: Fan out lifecycle calls across contexts; report closed when no contexts remain.
src/browser/Browser.ts.shouldNot: Perform fetches or DOM work directly.

src/browser/BrowserContext.ts.summary: Browser context owning pages, a cookie container, response cache, preflight cache, and module cache. close() aborts/destroys its pages and clears caches (forbidding closing the default context); provides newPage, waitUntilComplete, abort.
src/browser/BrowserContext.ts.should: Clear caches and cookies on close; fan lifecycle out to pages.
src/browser/BrowserContext.ts.shouldNot: Allow the default context to be closed directly.

src/browser/BrowserPage.ts.summary: Browser page wrapping a main BrowserFrame, virtual console, and viewport. Delegates content/url/navigation (goto/goBack/goForward/goSteps/reload), evaluate/evaluateModule, waitUntilComplete/waitForNavigation, abort, and close to the main frame; setViewport dispatches resize.
src/browser/BrowserPage.ts.should: Forward lifecycle/navigation to mainFrame; fire resize on viewport change.
src/browser/BrowserPage.ts.shouldNot: Own async tasks directly (the frame does).

src/browser/BrowserFrame.ts.summary: Browser frame owning a BrowserWindow, an AsyncTaskManager, child frames, history, and navigation listeners. Exposes content/url getters/setters, waitUntilComplete (awaits its task manager + child frames), waitForNavigation, abort (aborts manager + children), evaluate/evaluateModule, and history navigation via BrowserFrameNavigator.
src/browser/BrowserFrame.ts.outputs: window, asyncTaskManager, childFrames; Response Promises for navigation.
src/browser/BrowserFrame.ts.should: Aggregate async completion/abort across itself and child frames; observe exceptions when configured.
src/browser/BrowserFrame.ts.shouldNot: Perform network I/O itself beyond delegating to navigator/fetch.

src/browser/BrowserSettingsFactory.ts.summary: Merges user-supplied optional browser settings over DefaultBrowserSettings (including nested navigation/navigator/device/timer/fetch/debug objects) into a complete IBrowserSettings.

src/browser/utilities/BrowserPageUtility.summary: Static page helpers: getFrames flattens the frame tree; closePage marks the page closed, removes it from its context, closes the virtual console, and destroys the main frame (which destroys its async task manager).
src/browser/utilities/BrowserPageUtility.should: Tear down frames when closing a page.

src/browser/utilities/BrowserFrameFactor.summary: Static frame lifecycle helpers. createChildFrame constructs and links a child frame; destroyFrame marks a frame closed, recursively destroys child frames, awaits the AsyncTaskManager.destroy(), disconnects the exception observer, destroys the window, and clears opener/navigation references.
src/browser/utilities/BrowserFrameFactor.should: Destroy async task manager and window on teardown; unlink from parent.
src/browser/utilities/BrowserFrameFactor.shouldNot: Destroy a frame twice; leave child frames or listeners attached.

src/browser/utilities/BrowserFrameNaviga.summary: Navigation engine. navigate() handles hash/javascript:/about: schemes, cross-origin/frame validation, history push, swaps in a fresh Window + AsyncTaskManager, destroys the previous window/manager, performs the document Fetch with a timeout AbortController, enforces X-Frame-Options, and writes response HTML under requestAnimationFrame. navigateBack/Forward/Steps/reload drive history.
src/browser/utilities/BrowserFrameNaviga.inputs: { windowClass, frame, url, goToOptions, method, formData, disableHistory }.
src/browser/utilities/BrowserFrameNaviga.should: Track navigation as ready-state + async tasks/timer; resolve navigation listeners; abort on timeout.
src/browser/utilities/BrowserFrameNaviga.shouldNot: Leak the old window/task manager or the timeout timer; navigate a destroyed frame.

src/browser/utilities/BrowserExceptionOb.summary: Process-level uncaughtException/unhandledRejection listener that routes errors to the owning Window (matching by window.Error/DOMException instance), dispatching an ErrorEvent; exits the process if no other handlers exist. observe/disconnect manage per-window subscription and shared listener refcount.
src/browser/utilities/BrowserExceptionOb.should: Attach/detach process listeners with refcounting; target the right window.
src/browser/utilities/BrowserExceptionOb.shouldNot: Handle unhandledRejection in the uncaughtException path; leak process listeners after all windows disconnect.

src/browser/utilities/BrowserFrameScript.summary: Evaluates code/VM Scripts (evaluate) and modules (evaluateModule, via ModuleFactory / ECMAScript/JSON/CSS modules) in a frame's window context; throws if the frame's window has been destroyed.
src/browser/utilities/BrowserFrameScript.inputs: frame, script string|Script or module options { url, type, code }.
src/browser/utilities/BrowserFrameScript.should: Run within the frame window's VM context.

src/browser/detached-browser/DetachedBro.summary: Detached Browser used when a Window is constructed without a Browser. Holds the window class, settings, optional exception observer, and a single default DetachedBrowserContext; supports close/abort/waitUntilComplete/newPage but forbids new incognito contexts.
src/browser/detached-browser/DetachedBro.should: Mirror Browser lifecycle for a standalone Window.

src/browser/detached-browser/DetachedBro.summary: Detached browser context with cookie container, response/preflight/module caches, and a single default page. close aborts/destroys pages and clears caches (forbidding closing the default context); newPage/waitUntilComplete/abort as in BrowserContext.
src/browser/detached-browser/DetachedBro.shouldNot: Allow closing the default context directly.

src/browser/detached-browser/DetachedBro.summary: Detached browser page wrapping a DetachedBrowserFrame; like BrowserPage but close() also closes the owning browser when it is the sole page (since detached contexts have no external references). Delegates navigation/evaluate/lifecycle to the main frame.
src/browser/detached-browser/DetachedBro.should: Cascade close to the browser for the last detached page; fire resize on viewport change.
src/browser/detached-browser/DetachedBro.shouldNot: Leave an orphaned browser/context after closing the only page.

src/browser/detached-browser/DetachedBro.summary: Detached browser frame; like BrowserFrame but the window is injected externally and may be absent after teardown (accessors throw when window is unset). Owns an AsyncTaskManager, history, and navigation listeners; waitUntilComplete/abort aggregate across child frames.
src/browser/detached-browser/DetachedBro.should: Guard content/url access against a destroyed window; aggregate async completion/abort.

src/window/BrowserWindow.ts.summary: The window global for a frame and its DOM/timer/fetch host. Sets up the VM context, extends web-API classes, manages document ready-state/load, and implements setTimeout/clearTimeout/setInterval/clearInterval/requestAnimationFrame/cancelAnimationFrame/queueMicrotask (each registered with the frame's AsyncTaskManager and guarded by timer-loop/max limits and error capture), fetch/XHR entry points, and [destroy]() teardown of observers/sockets/document.
src/window/BrowserWindow.ts.inputs: browserFrame, options { url }; script-facing global APIs.
src/window/BrowserWindow.ts.outputs: timer/immediate IDs; document, navigator, location, storage, etc.
src/window/BrowserWindow.ts.should: Track every timer/immediate/microtask/fetch in the async task manager; short-circuit when closed; enforce timer-loop and max-timeout limits; capture callback errors.
src/window/BrowserWindow.ts.shouldNot: Run timers/tasks after the window is closed; leak tasks on the async manager.

src/window/Window.ts.summary: Public Window entry point. Constructs a DetachedBrowser+page+frame under the hood, applies viewport options, extends BrowserWindow, wires the frame's window reference and exception observer, and exposes the happyDOM DetachedWindowAPI.
src/window/Window.ts.inputs: options { width, height, url, console, settings }.
src/window/Window.ts.should: Bootstrap a standalone browser environment for a Window.

src/window/GlobalWindow.ts.summary: Window subclass that also exposes Node.js globals (Array, Object, Promise, Buffer, etc.) directly on the window and overrides evaluateScript to use eval and setupVMContext to a no-op (runs in the current realm rather than a VM context).
src/window/GlobalWindow.ts.outputs: Window with JS globals; eval-based script evaluation.
src/window/GlobalWindow.ts.should: Provide a non-VM global window sharing the host realm.
src/window/GlobalWindow.ts.shouldNot: Set up a separate VM context.

src/window/CrossOriginBrowserWindow.ts.summary: Restricted window proxy exposed across origins (iframes/popups). Allows only self/top/parent/opener/closed/blur/focus/close/postMessage; accessing location throws a security DOMException. Forwards permitted calls to the target window.
src/window/CrossOriginBrowserWindow.ts.should: Block cross-origin property access and forward safe operations.

src/window/WindowBrowserContext.ts.summary: Internal bridge from a Window to its browser internals without exposing the Browser to scripts. Keeps a static window-internalId -> BrowserFrame map and returns the frame, page, context, browser, settings, or AsyncTaskManager for a window; manages the window/frame relation lifecycle.
src/window/WindowBrowserContext.ts.should: Provide safe internal access and return null when the frame is gone.
src/window/WindowBrowserContext.ts.shouldNot: Expose the Browser as a script-reachable property.

src/window/DetachedWindowAPI.ts.summary: The window.happyDOM control surface for detached windows. Exposes settings/virtualConsolePrinter, waitUntilComplete (+deprecated whenAsyncComplete), abort (+deprecated cancelAsync), close, setURL, and viewport/size setters, all delegating to the browser frame/page.
src/window/DetachedWindowAPI.ts.should: Delegate async-lifecycle and viewport control to the frame/page.
src/window/DetachedWindowAPI.ts.shouldNot: Contain its own async task logic.

src/window/WindowContextClassExtender.ts.summary: Extends a window with per-window subclasses of web-API implementations (Document, Text, Headers, Request, Response, AbortController, AbortSignal, FormData, XMLHttpRequest, URL, WebSocket, etc.), attaching the window to each class prototype so instances can reach their browser context.
src/window/WindowContextClassExtender.ts.outputs: window.* constructor classes bound to the window.
src/window/WindowContextClassExtender.ts.should: Bind every exposed class prototype to its owning window.

src/window/WindowPageOpenUtility.ts.summary: Implements window.open. Parses window features, resolves the target frame (_self/_top/_parent/_blank/named), navigates it via goto, sets popup/viewport/name and opener relations, and returns a same-origin window or a CrossOriginBrowserWindow (or null for noopener/noreferrer).
src/window/WindowPageOpenUtility.ts.should: Honor noopener/noreferrer and cross-origin restrictions when returning the opened window.
src/window/WindowPageOpenUtility.ts.shouldNot: Expose a same-origin window reference when noopener/noreferrer is set.
